user.title: Rust+
user.description: How would you like to sign in to your Rust+ account?

qr.title: Scan with the Steam Mobile app
qr.description:
Open the Steam Mobile app, scan the code below, and approve the sign-in — then click **Submit**.

{qr_image}

qr_wait.title: Signing in…
qr_wait.description: Waiting for approval and registering for push notifications…

qr_failed.title: Sign-in didn't complete
qr_failed.description: The Steam QR sign-in failed or timed out. Click **Submit** to show a new code and try again.

manual.description: Paste the FCM credentials JSON from the Rust+ companion browser extension.

reconfigure.title: Refresh Rust+ credentials
reconfigure.description: How would you like to refresh your account credentials? Your paired devices are kept.

reconfigure_manual.title: Refresh Rust+ credentials
reconfigure_manual.description: Paste the new FCM credentials JSON. This replaces the stored credentials for this account and reconnects the push listener. Your paired devices are kept.

confirm_discovery.title: Add Rust Server
confirm_discovery.description: A Rust server was paired via the Rust+ app. Do you want to add it to Home Assistant?

progress.awaiting_scan: Waiting for you to scan the Steam QR code and approve the sign-in in the Steam Mobile app. This window stays open for a few minutes, then registers for push notifications automatically.

abort.qr_begin_failed: Couldn't start the Steam sign-in. Please try again.
abort.reconfigure_successful: Credentials updated. Your paired devices were kept.
abort.reconfigure_account_only: Only the Rust+ account entry holds FCM credentials. Reconfigure the account entry, not a server.

device_paired.title: Rust+ Device Paired: {name}
device_paired.description: A new {type} ({name}, ID: {id}) was paired via the Rust+ app, but it couldn't be matched to a configured server yet. Make sure the server is added to Home Assistant and loaded, then re-pair the device — it will be added automatically.

rename_device.title: Action Required: Rename {name}
rename_device.description:
You successfully paired the {type} **{name}** (ID: {id}).

By default, Facepunch only provides generic names like 'Smart Alarm' during pairing.

**Please go to your Home Assistant Devices dashboard, click on this newly paired entity, and rename it to a friendly name (like 'Front Door Alarm') so you can easily identify it!**
